old_claim.note: The telescope enabled recorded celestial observation programs such as Galileo's observations of the Moon, Jupiter, Venus, sunspots, and the Milky Way.

new_claim_absent: No direct telescope edge remains because observatory_instrument_programs is now scoped to Tycho Brahe's pre-telescopic Uraniborg/Stjerneborg instrument program rather than Galileo's later telescopic observations.

source_shape.source_locator: History section: the portal identifies Tycho as the most important observational astronomer until the telescope's invention in 1608 and describes his pre-telescopic quadrants, sextants, armillary spheres, mountings, diopters, and measurement methods.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Tycho's Uraniborg/Stjerneborg instrument program predates the telescope and used improved naked-eye instruments and measurement methods.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A telescope can enable later Galileo-style observing, but it cannot be a direct prerequisite for the corrected Uraniborg instrument-program scope.

ontology_before: Observatory Instrument Programs blended Tycho's pre-telescopic observatory program with Galileo's telescope-based observation program.
ontology_after: Uraniborg Observatory Instrument Programs is scoped to Tycho's pre-telescopic, calibrated naked-eye observatory program, with telescope-based observation represented elsewhere.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: observatory_instrument_programs no longer depends directly on telescope.
would_reject_if: The node were broadened back to include Galileo's telescope-based observations as the same scoped technology.
A separate telescope-observatory program node were added and should keep a telescope edge.
A source showed Uraniborg's 1582 instrument program used telescopes before 1608.
short_reason: Tycho's Uraniborg program was pre-telescopic, so telescope is outside the corrected scope.
